---
title: "Test_Framework"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-29"
---

# PURPOSE
# This notebook implements the TRAINING PIPELINE only (no external test set).
# It covers: data loading â†’ preprocessing â†’ individual filtering â†’ forward selection â†’ MINT modelling.
# Use Test_Framework(Reproducibility).Rmd to extend with the HGMA test set and external validation.

```{r}
# Clear the workspace to avoid stale variables from previous runs
rm(list=ls())
# Load all helper functions (preprocessing, filtering, modelling, visualisation)
source("Function_Helpers.R")
```

# Packages installation
```{r}
# req_pkgs: character vector of all packages required by this pipeline
# - dplyr:                   data manipulation (filter, select, mutate, bind_rows)
# - mixOmics:                PLS-DA, MINT sPLS-DA, CLR transformation, perf(), tune()
# - curatedMetagenomicData:  access to curated gut microbiome study datasets (CMD v3.16.1)
# - SummarizedExperiment:    data container used by curatedMetagenomicData (assay(), colData())
# - stringr:                 string manipulation (str_detect, str_sub, regex)
# - testthat:                unit testing utilities (used in validation checks)
# - tibble:                  tibble data frames (used by some helper functions)
# - purrr:                   functional programming (map, imap, map_lgl)
req_pkgs = c('dplyr', 'mixOmics', 'curatedMetagenomicData', 'SummarizedExperiment',
             'stringr', 'testthat', 'tibble', 'stringr', 'purrr',
             'openxlsx')  # openxlsx: required by run_mint() to export selected taxa + error rates to Excel

install_if_missing(req_pkgs)
```

## Data Loading
```{r}
# study_names: CMD query strings for the 5 training cohorts
# Format: "<AuthorYear>.<data_type>"
# The 5 studies span diverse populations (Chinese, Swedish, Indian, American):
#   FengQ_2015       â€“ colorectal cancer cohort (Chinese); contains T2D/Healthy
#   KarlssonFH_2013  â€“ Swedish T2D cohort; the canonical T2D microbiome study
#   LiJ_2014         â€“ large-scale Chinese metagenome reference cohort
#   YuJ_2015         â€“ colorectal cancer cohort (Chinese); also contains T2D/Healthy
#   SankaranarayananK_2015 â€“ Indian cohort
study_names <- c("FengQ_2015.relative_abundance", "KarlssonFH_2013.relative_abundance", "LiJ_2014.relative_abundance",
                 "YuJ_2015.relative_abundance", "SankaranarayananK_2015.relative_abundance")

# Load and process each study
# abundance_lst: named list (length 5); each element is a data.frame:
#   rows = samples (sample IDs as row names)
#   cols = taxa (species names as column names, e.g. "Bacteroides uniformis")
# counts=TRUE retrieves raw counts (not pre-normalised relative abundances)
# rownames="short" assigns short sample IDs rather than full accession strings
# t() transposes from taxaĂ—samples to samplesĂ—taxa orientation
# check.names=FALSE preserves special characters and spaces in taxa names
abundance_lst <- lapply(study_names, function(study) {
  rawdata <- curatedMetagenomicData(study, dryrun = FALSE, counts = TRUE, rownames = "short")
  raw_tse <- rawdata[[1]]  # extract the SummarizedExperiment object
  abund <- assay(raw_tse, "relative_abundance")  # taxa Ă— samples matrix
  
  # Joyce: added checknames =false to prevent issues with inconsistent colnames later
  as.data.frame(t(abund), check.names = FALSE)  # transpose â†’ samples Ă— taxa
})

## output data: keys are study name, each col is taxa name
# meta_lst: named list (length 5); each element is a data.frame:
#   rows = samples (sample IDs as row names)
#   cols = all CMD metadata columns (includes 'disease', 'gender', 'body_site', etc.)
# Extract metadata for each study
meta_lst <- lapply(study_names, function(study) {
  rawdata <- curatedMetagenomicData(study, dryrun = FALSE, counts = TRUE, rownames = "short")
  raw_tse <- rawdata[[1]]
  as.data.frame(colData(raw_tse), check.names = FALSE)  # sample metadata from SummarizedExperiment
})

```




```{r}
# study_names_v2: shortened study names (strip ".relative_abundance" suffix)
# e.g. "FengQ_2015.relative_abundance" â†’ "FengQ_2015"
# Used as list names for abundance_lst and meta_lst so they are human-readable
# and match the format used as the study label in modelling.
study_names_v2 = c()
for (i in 1:length(strsplit(study_names, "\\.")) ) {
  # strsplit splits on "."; take the first element (the study name before the dot)
  study_names_v2[i] = strsplit(study_names, "\\.")[[i]][1]
}

# Apply shortened names to both lists (enables name-based access downstream)
names(abundance_lst) <- study_names_v2
names(meta_lst) <- study_names_v2
```


## Data Processing
```{r}
# keep_cols: additional metadata columns to retain alongside 'disease'
#   "gender"    â€“ sex of the participant (used in downstream characterisation)
#   "body_site" â€“ sampling location (all should be stool for gut microbiome studies)
keep_cols <-c("gender", "body_site")

# diseases: the two disease classes we are discriminating
#   "Healthy" â€“ Normal Glucose Tolerance (NGT) / control participants
#   "T2D"     â€“ Type 2 Diabetes participants
diseases <- c("Healthy", "T2D")

# run_preprocessing() is the main preprocessing entry point (Function_Helpers.R).
# For 5 studies it routes to preprocess_multiple() â†’ preprocess_single() per study â†’ integrate_filter()
#
# Parameters used:
#   percent = 0.01    â†’ remove taxa contributing < 0.01% of total abundance (low-count removal)
#   offset_value = 1  â†’ add 1 to all counts before CLR (standard for count data to handle zeros)
#   threshold = 1     â†’ keep taxa present in at least 1 study (effectively: keep all)
#
# res: named nested list; keys = study short names (e.g. "FengQ_2015")
#   res[[study_name]]$abund â€“ CLR-transformed abundance data frame (samples Ă— surviving taxa)
#   res[[study_name]]$meta  â€“ filtered metadata (disease, gender, body_site columns)
res<- run_preprocessing(abundance_list = abundance_lst, meta_list = meta_lst, diseases = diseases, keep_cols = keep_cols, percent = 0.01, offset_value = 1, threshold = 1)

```

# Model Development and Training
## Data Integration
### Individual filtering
```{r}
# individual_filtering() (Layer 2): removes studies where any disease class is too
# hard to discriminate (per-class BER > err_thres at ncomp components).
#
# INPUT:  res â€“ preprocessed study list from run_preprocessing()
# OUTPUT: result_individual â€“ filtered study list (same structure as res, failing studies removed)
#
# Parameters:
#   dist      = "centroids.dist" â€“ nearest-centroid classification rule for PLS-DA CV
#   ncomp     = 2                â€“ evaluate at 2 PLS-DA components
#   err_thres = 0.5              â€“ discard any study where a class BER > 50%
#                                  (NOTE: thesis specifies 0.7; reconcile before full_framework.Rmd)
#
# CMD_filtered = readRDS("res")  # [ORIGINAL] was loaded from disk; now kept in memory
result_individual <- individual_filtering(res, dist="centroids.dist", ncomp=2, err_thres = 0.5)
print("Individual Filtering â€” selected studies:")
names(result_individual)  # prints the names of studies that passed the filter

```

### Forward selection
```{r}
# forward_selection() (Layer 3): greedy algorithm to find the optimal SUBSET of
# studies to integrate. Studies are added one-at-a-time; a study is accepted only
# if adding it reduces the multi-study MINT BER.
#
# INPUT:  res â€“ preprocessed study list (NOTE: uses res, not the individually-filtered result;
#               forward selection implicitly handles noisy studies via BER)
# OUTPUT: result_forward â€“ named list of the selected study objects (abund + meta)
#
# Parameters:
#   dist  = "centroids.dist" â€“ classification rule for MINT evaluation
#   ncomp = 2                â€“ number of MINT components
result_forward = forward_selection(res, dist="centroids.dist", ncomp=2)
print("Forward Selection â€” selected studies:")
names(result_forward)  # prints which studies were selected in the final combination

```

## Modelling
### Individual Filtering â€” MINT Model
```{r}
## Assemble integrative dataset from individually-filtered studies

# Add 'study' column to each study's metadata for provenance tracking after bind_rows
for (name in names(result_individual)) {
  result_individual[[name]]$meta$study = strsplit(name, '\\.')[[1]][1]
}

# result_df_individual: all individually-filtered studies stacked into one data frame
# $abund â€“ combined CLR abundance (rows = all samples, cols = taxa)
# $meta  â€“ combined metadata (rows = all samples, includes $study column)
result_df_individual = bind_rows(result_individual)

# p_selected_individual: training abundance matrix for the individual-filtering strategy.
# Used in align_columns() when aligning with the HGMA test set in Reproducibility notebook.
p_selected_individual <- result_df_individual$abund

# Prepare run_mint() inputs for the individual-filtering strategy
# study must exist in the calling environment â€” run_mint() reads it directly
X     <- result_df_individual$abund
Y     <- as.factor(result_df_individual$meta$disease)
study <- as.factor(result_df_individual$meta$study)

# run_mint() with label="individual" â†’ outputs saved as:
#   output_files/individual_selected_taxa.xlsx
#   (plots saved by plot_mint_results below)
mint_results_individual <- run_mint(X = X, Y = Y, ncomp = 2, dist = "centroids.dist",
                                    label = "individual")

# plot_mint_results() with label="individual" â†’ saves:
#   output_files/individual_scoreplot.png
#   output_files/individual_loadings_comp1.png
#   output_files/individual_loadings_comp2.png
plot_mint_results(mint_results_individual, Y = Y, study = study, label = "individual")
```

```{r}
## Confusion matrix â€” Individual Filtering training set
# predict() on training data gives in-sample performance (optimistic upper bound).
predict_individual <- predict(mint_results_individual$model, newdata = X,
                              dist = "centroids.dist", study.test = study)

# conf_mat_individual_c1/c2: confusion matrices at component 1 and component 2
conf_mat_individual_c1 = get.confusion_matrix(truth = result_df_individual$meta$disease,
                                              predicted = predict_individual$class$centroids.dist[, 1])
conf_mat_individual_c2 = get.confusion_matrix(truth = result_df_individual$meta$disease,
                                              predicted = predict_individual$class$centroids.dist[, 2])
conf_mat_individual_c1
conf_mat_individual_c2
```

### Forward Selection â€” MINT Model
```{r}
## Assemble integrative dataset from forward-selected studies

# Add 'study' column to each study's metadata for provenance tracking after bind_rows
for (name in names(result_forward)) {
  result_forward[[name]]$meta$study = strsplit(name, '\\.')[[1]][1]
}

# result_df_forward: all forward-selected studies stacked into one data frame
# $abund â€“ combined CLR abundance (rows = all samples, cols = taxa)
# $meta  â€“ combined metadata (rows = all samples, includes $study column)
result_df_forward = bind_rows(result_forward)

# p_selected_forward: training abundance matrix for the forward-selection strategy.
# Used in align_columns() when aligning with the HGMA test set.
p_selected_forward <- result_df_forward$abund

# Prepare run_mint() inputs for the forward-selection strategy
X     <- result_df_forward$abund
Y     <- as.factor(result_df_forward$meta$disease)
study <- as.factor(result_df_forward$meta$study)

# run_mint() with label="forward" â†’ outputs saved as:
#   output_files/forward_selected_taxa.xlsx
mint_results_forward <- run_mint(X = X, Y = Y, ncomp = 2, dist = "centroids.dist",
                                 label = "forward")

# plot_mint_results() with label="forward" â†’ saves score plot + loadings for forward strategy
plot_mint_results(mint_results_forward, Y = Y, study = study, label = "forward")
```

```{r}
## Confusion matrix â€” Forward Selection training set
predict_forward <- predict(mint_results_forward$model, newdata = X,
                           dist = "centroids.dist", study.test = study)

conf_mat_forward_c1 = get.confusion_matrix(truth = result_df_forward$meta$disease,
                                           predicted = predict_forward$class$centroids.dist[, 1])
conf_mat_forward_c2 = get.confusion_matrix(truth = result_df_forward$meta$disease,
                                           predicted = predict_forward$class$centroids.dist[, 2])
conf_mat_forward_c1
conf_mat_forward_c2
```

## Strategy Selection
```{r}
# Compare BER at component 2 for both strategies.
# Lower BER â†’ better discrimination â†’ selected as the final model.
# BER[2] indexes the component 2 row of perf$global.error$BER.
ber_individual <- mint_results_individual$performance$global.error$BER[2]
ber_forward    <- mint_results_forward$performance$global.error$BER[2]

cat("BER (comp 2) â€” Individual Filtering:", ber_individual, "\n")
cat("BER (comp 2) â€” Forward Selection:   ", ber_forward,    "\n")

# selected_set: the winning strategy's combined data frame (abund + meta)
# search_f:     string label used to identify which strategy was selected
if (ber_individual <= ber_forward) {
  selected_set <- result_df_individual
  search_f     <- "individual"
} else {
  selected_set <- result_df_forward
  search_f     <- "forward"
}

cat("Selected strategy:", search_f, "\n")
```

## Pathway a
```{r}
# Biological interpretation stub â€” to be completed.
# Will use selected taxa from selected_set's MINT model to:
#   - Map selected taxa to MetaCyc/KEGG pathways
#   - Visualise contribution to each component
```

## Generalization
```{r}
#### apply align_columns in selected_set and testing set.
## test set1: PRJEB1786 (Swedish cohort)
test1 = readRDS("PRJEB1786.rds")

# HGMA data is already relative abundance (not raw counts), so offset must be
# the smallest non-zero value in the data â€” NOT 1 (which is for count data).
x1      <- unlist(test1$abund)
offset1 <- min(x1[x1 > 0])
cat("PRJEB1786 offset:", offset1, "\n")

processed1 <- preprocess_single(
  meta         = test1$meta,
  abund        = test1$abund,
  diseases     = c("T2D", "NGT"),
  keep_cols    = NULL,   # HGMA has no gender/body_site columns
  percent      = 0.01,
  offset_value = offset1,
  CLR          = TRUE
)

# [FALLBACK] If preprocess_single() fails for HGMA, use these two calls directly:
# filtered1 <- low_count_removal(test1$abund, 0.01)$data.filter
# test1$abund <- clr.transformation(filtered1, offset1)

processed1$meta <- processed1$meta %>%
  mutate(disease = ifelse(disease == "NGT", "Healthy", disease))

test1$abund <- processed1$data
test1$meta  <- processed1$meta
```

```{r}
## test set2: PRJNA422434 (Chinese cohort)
test2 = readRDS("PRJNA422434.rds")

# PRJNA422434 uses "NGT" (Normal Glucose Tolerance) as the Healthy label.
# Recode to "Healthy" BEFORE preprocess_single() so filter_meta() retains these samples.
test2$meta$disease <- ifelse(test2$meta$disease == "NGT", "Healthy", test2$meta$disease)

x2      <- unlist(test2$abund)
offset2 <- min(x2[x2 > 0])
cat("PRJNA422434 offset:", offset2, "\n")

processed2 <- preprocess_single(
  meta         = test2$meta,
  abund        = test2$abund,
  diseases     = c("T2D", "Healthy"),
  keep_cols    = NULL,
  percent      = 0.01,
  offset_value = offset2,
  CLR          = TRUE
)

test2$abund <- processed2$data
test2$meta  <- processed2$meta
```

### Testing outcome 1 (PRJEB1786)
```{r}
## get overlapped set
# align_columns() strips "species: " prefixes, resolves HGMA "/" aliases,
# and returns $training / $testing restricted to their common taxa columns.
combined_obj1 = align_columns(p_selected=selected_set$abund, p_test=test1$abund)

# Re-train MINT on common-taxa training subset.
# model_mode = "testing" skips the xlsx save (we only want the fitted model here).
X_train1  <- combined_obj1$training
Y_train1  <- as.factor(selected_set$meta$disease)
study_train1 <- as.factor(selected_set$meta$study)

# include tuning
#mint_results_test1 <- run_mint(X = X_train1, Y = Y_train1, ncomp = 2,
#                               dist = "centroids.dist",
#                               label = "prjeb1786", model_mode = "testing")
# no tuning, pure mint.plsda
mint_results_test1 = mint.plsda(X = X_train1, Y = Y_train1, 
                                study = study_train1, ncomp = 2)

# Predict on HGMA test set.
# study.test must be a factor; all test samples belong to a single external study.
predict_test1 <- predict(#mint_results_test1$model,
                         mint_results_test1,
                         newdata     = combined_obj1$testing,
                         dist        = "centroids.dist",
                         study.test  = factor(rep("PRJEB1786", nrow(combined_obj1$testing))))

# Confusion matrices at comp1 and comp2
cm_test1_c1 <- get.confusion_matrix(truth     = test1$meta$disease,
                                    predicted = predict_test1$class$centroids.dist[, 1])
cm_test1_c2 <- get.confusion_matrix(truth     = test1$meta$disease,
                                    predicted = predict_test1$class$centroids.dist[, 2])

cat("=== PRJEB1786 â€” Component 1 ===\n"); print(cm_test1_c1)
cat("=== PRJEB1786 â€” Component 2 ===\n"); print(cm_test1_c2)

# Compute specificity, sensitivity, and per-class BER from the confusion matrices.
# Convention: T2D = positive class, Healthy = negative class.
# cm rows = truth, cm cols = predicted; get.confusion_matrix() returns a 2Ă—2 matrix.
#
#           Predicted:  Healthy    T2D
# Truth:  Healthy  [  TN         FP  ]
#         T2D      [  FN         TP  ]
#
# Sensitivity (Recall, T2D) = TP / (TP + FN)
# Specificity (Healthy)     = TN / (TN + FP)
# Per-class BER_T2D         = FN / (TP + FN)   [= 1 - Sensitivity]
# Per-class BER_Healthy     = FP / (TN + FP)   [= 1 - Specificity]

compute_metrics <- function(cm, label) {
  # cm: 2Ă—2 confusion matrix with rows = truth, cols = predicted
  # Row/col order: alphabetical (Healthy first, T2D second)
  TN <- cm["Healthy", "predicted.as.Healthy"]
  FP <- cm["Healthy", "predicted.as.T2D"]
  FN <- cm["T2D",     "predicted.as.Healthy"]
  TP <- cm["T2D",     "predicted.as.T2D"]

  sensitivity   <- TP / (TP + FN)
  specificity   <- TN / (TN + FP)
  ber_T2D       <- FN / (TP + FN)         # per-class error for T2D
  ber_Healthy   <- FP / (TN + FP)         # per-class error for Healthy
  overall_BER   <- (ber_T2D + ber_Healthy) / 2

  cat(sprintf("--- %s ---\n", label))
  cat(sprintf("  Sensitivity (T2D):     %.4f\n", sensitivity))
  cat(sprintf("  Specificity (Healthy): %.4f\n", specificity))
  cat(sprintf("  BER (T2D class):       %.4f\n", ber_T2D))
  cat(sprintf("  BER (Healthy class):   %.4f\n", ber_Healthy))
  cat(sprintf("  Overall BER:           %.4f\n", overall_BER))
}

compute_metrics(cm_test1_c1, "PRJEB1786 â€” Comp 1")
compute_metrics(cm_test1_c2, "PRJEB1786 â€” Comp 2")
```

### Testing outcome 2 (PRJNA422434)
```{r}
## get overlapped set
combined_obj2 = align_columns(p_selected=selected_set$abund, p_test=test2$abund)

# Re-train MINT on common-taxa training subset for PRJNA422434.
X_train2     <- combined_obj2$training
Y_train2     <- as.factor(selected_set$meta$disease)
study_train2 <- as.factor(selected_set$meta$study)

#mint_results_test2 <- run_mint(X = X_train2, Y = Y_train2, ncomp = 2,
#                               dist = "centroids.dist",
#                               label = "prjna422434", model_mode = "testing")
mint_results_test2 = mint.plsda(X = X_train2, Y = Y_train2, 
                                study = study_train2, ncomp = 2)

# Predict on HGMA test set 2.
predict_test2 <- predict(#mint_results_test2$model,
                         mint_results_test2, 
                         newdata    = combined_obj2$testing,
                         dist       = "centroids.dist",
                         study.test = factor(rep("PRJNA422434", nrow(combined_obj2$testing))))

# Confusion matrices at comp1 and comp2
cm_test2_c1 <- get.confusion_matrix(truth     = test2$meta$disease,
                                    predicted = predict_test2$class$centroids.dist[, 1])
cm_test2_c2 <- get.confusion_matrix(truth     = test2$meta$disease,
                                    predicted = predict_test2$class$centroids.dist[, 2])

cat("=== PRJNA422434 â€” Component 1 ===\n"); print(cm_test2_c1)
cat("=== PRJNA422434 â€” Component 2 ===\n"); print(cm_test2_c2)

compute_metrics(cm_test2_c1, "PRJNA422434 â€” Comp 1")
compute_metrics(cm_test2_c2, "PRJNA422434 â€” Comp 2")
```
